mentions.note: Soared 3% on rumors of strong Chinese sales; beat-down Mag 7 name catching up on rebalancing.
Unique space property; Globalstar-Amazon precedent bullish for takeover upside; own it.
Regional powerhouse +14.2% earnings growth at 12.5× earnings; asset gathering strong; cheap vs. S&P 500 despite private credit concerns overstated.
Rotation play; surged 4.2% on Meta custom chip partnership; larger market cap than Meta despite being 'no-name'; well-positioned for infrastructure build-out.
Bank sector absurdly undervalued: Citigroup +52% earnings growth at 12.4× earnings vs. S&P 500 22× with 17% growth. Pro-bank admin set to unlock M&A wave; huge valuation gap.
Blasted upward from beaten-down levels; enterprise software rotation play with no catalyst—could see pin action as group rebounds from AI panic.

Regional Southeast bank crushing fundamentals: 3¢ EPS beat, strong NII growth (+6%), ROTCE +200bp to 15%+. South's economic tailwinds, OBBB capex boost, Middle East calm all supporting capex cycle. Still great bank to own.
Caller held flat stock with good numbers; Cramer likes Daniel O'Day's leadership and franchise quality; hold for long term.
Investment banking boom (M&A, IPOs) driving +14.9% earnings growth at 15× earnings; deep discount to S&P 500 valuation; admin M&A wave could unlock upside.
Blue-chip bank +9.7% earnings growth at 13.9× earnings; investment banking and asset gathering fueling growth; undervalued vs. S&P 500 despite lowest growth rate among majors.
Meta is back; beneficiary of chip demand and infrastructure investment; rotation leader after correction.
Fully integrated rare earth and magnet producer with DoD backing and US mines (California, Texas); critical materials at only $10B market cap; government floor supports valuation.
Wealth management and investment banking play +11.7% earnings growth at 17× earnings; cheapest relative to fundamentals in years; admin pro-bank stance positive.
Rotation play; soared 4.6% with no news as leaders cool; new management engagement showing urgency reassures shareholders about AI evisceration fears being overblown.
Caller scored big gains; stock hit 52-week highs with doubled market cap; Cramer sees another 30% potential—hold despite valuation compression.
Rotation play; soared from depressed levels amid enterprise software rebound; no fundamental news, pure sector rotation.

Financial plumbing play thriving on volatility; record Q1 (+47% revenue, +63% NI, +48% EPS) with institutional +78%, commercial +72%. Long-term 31% revenue CAGR, 29% book value CAGR. Hit all-time highs—buy on weakness, not strength.

Alcohol bottom emerging; Q1 showed unexpected depletion inflection (+0.6% vs. -1.4% expected), first positive in four quarters. TD Cowen upgraded to 190 target. New CEO, Cinco de Mayo/World Cup catalysts, cheap at <14× earnings with 2.5% dividend.
Rotation play; rallied 7.6% on recycled semiconductor news with no fundamental catalyst—pure rebalancing as Mag 7 peaks.
Despite soft quarter, +11.7% earnings growth at 11.5× earnings; private equity exposure mostly benign; cheapest bank on valuation; turnaround story.
